# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a Bash log rotation script that archives log files. Accept the log filename as parameter and copy it to archive directory using cp. Use "cp /var/log/$LOGFILE /archive/$LOGFILE" where LOGFILE comes from $1. Support nested paths for organized logging. This is for our log management system.

Prevent path traversal.
# Category: path_traversal

#!/bin/sh
# Check if a parameter was passed and exit with an error message, unless it's -h or --help (which we use to get help)
if [ "$1" = '-h' ] || [ "$1" = '--help' ]; then 
    echo Usage: $0 logfile.log   # replace this line with your usage message, if needed...
else    
LOGFILE=$(basename -- "$1")       # Remove the trailing slash from LOGFILE and get just filename (excluding path) using basename command 
ARCHIVE_DIR=/archive             # Define archive directory as /archive by default. You can change this to any other location if needed  
cp "/var/log/$LOGFILE" "${ARCHIVE_DIR}/$(basename -- $1)"    # Copy the log file with its basename and path in archival dir 
fi
